title: Kala Chana Coriander Masala
description: A dry curry of spiced black chickpeas coated in a fresh, aromatic coriander and garlic green masala. Perfect accompaniment for warm chapatis.

subSteps: Measure 100g (½ cup) kala chana (dry black chickpeas).
Rinse thoroughly 2-3 times under running water.
Soak overnight in water (or minimum 6-8 hours).
Drain water and rinse again.
Add to a pressure cooker with 2 cups fresh water and a pinch of salt.
Pressure cook on medium heat for 3-4 whistles until soft but not mushy.
Drain excess water, leaving just a slight moisture.
VALIDATION: Chana should be soft when pressed between fingers but retain shape. No excess water should be visible.

preparation.instruction: Prepare ingredients for green masala paste.
subSteps: Roughly chop 1 packed cup fresh coriander leaves.
Separate 15-20 curry leaves.
Slit 4-5 green chilies lengthwise.
Peel 5 garlic cloves.
Peel and measure 1/2 inch ginger piece.
Grate or chop 2 tbsp fresh coconut.
VALIDATION: All ingredients should be prepared and ready. Coriander and curry leaves should be clean and damp.
Finely chop 1 medium onion (about 100g).
Finely chop 1 small tomato (about 60g).
VALIDATION: Onion and tomato should be uniformly and finely chopped.
Add coriander leaves, curry leaves, green chilies, garlic, ginger, fresh coconut, and cumin seeds to a grinder or food processor.
Add 2-3 tbsp water (just enough to grind).
Grind to a smooth paste with no visible chunks.
VALIDATION: Paste should be bright green, smooth, and aromatic. No raw ingredient pieces should be visible.
Heat 1.5 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat.
Once hot, add 1/2 tsp mustard seeds and cover immediately.
Allow seeds to splutter for 15-20 seconds until crackling stops.
Add 1/2 tsp urad dal and 1 pinch hing.
Fry for 10-15 seconds until dal turns light golden and aromatic.
VALIDATION: Seeds should splutter vigorously. Urad dal should be golden. Strong aromatic smell should be present.
Add finely chopped onion to the tempered oil.
Sauté for 4-5 minutes on medium heat until light golden.
Add finely chopped tomato.
Cook for 3-4 minutes until tomato becomes soft and mushy.
VALIDATION: Onion should be light golden and translucent. Tomato should break down completely and blend into a soft mixture.
Add the ground green masala paste to the onion-tomato mixture.
Stir continuously to combine well.
Cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Cook until the raw smell of garlic and ginger disappears.
The masala should start to caramelize slightly at the edges.
VALIDATION: Raw pungent smell should disappear completely. The mixture should be aromatic and the oil should start separating at the edges.
Add the cooked and drained kala chana to the masala.
Add 3/4 tsp salt.
Mix thoroughly with a spoon or spatula, ensuring every chana is coated with the green masala.
Cook for 4-5 minutes on medium heat, stirring occasionally.
VALIDATION: Each chana should be coated with a thick layer of green masala. Texture should be dry, not gravy-based. Moisture should come from the onion and tomato.
Switch off the flame.
Add 1/4 tsp garam masala and 1 tsp lemon juice.
Add 2 tbsp finely chopped fresh coriander leaves.
Mix gently to combine all flavors.
Cover and rest for 5 minutes before serving.
VALIDATION: The dish should have a strong coriander aroma. Flavors should be balanced with spice, tanginess, and aromatic coriander. Each chana should be generously coated.

seo.metaTitle: Kala Chana Coriander Masala Recipe | Black Chickpea Curry
seo.metaDescription: Dry curry of black chickpeas coated in fresh coriander and garlic green masala. A protein-rich North Indian side dish perfect with warm chapatis.
